metadata.title: Breeder profile web research - July 15 batch 6 c
metadata.notes: Public-web research only. Employer, breeder, applicant, licensee, agent, and legal-holder roles remain distinct; historical patents were not reassigned to later employers or successor programs; no external communication was made.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Berthold (Bob) Bergh was the University of California, Riverside avocado breeder from 1956 until 1991. UCR and patent records place program rights with The Regents of the University of California, not with Bergh personally; international commercial licenses such as Eurosemillas are license relationships and do not replace the filing-era owner.
profiles.headquarters: Riverside, California, United States (University of California, Riverside)
profiles.leadershipSummary: Bergh died in 2021. UCR identifies Mary Lu Arpaia and Eric Focht as current avocado-program leads carrying forward material that includes Bergh selections.

webResearchSources.label: UCR avocado release and program succession

profiles.webResearchNotes: Treat as a historical UCR institutional profile. Preserve The Regents as assignee where shown and evaluate each cultivar's patent, trademark, and territorial license separately.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Craig Chandler is a University of Florida professor emeritus and former strawberry breeder. Cultivar rights and licensing are administered through the University of Florida system and Florida Foundation Seed Producers as documented for each cultivar, rather than owned by Chandler personally.
profiles.headquarters: Gainesville, Florida, United States; strawberry breeding program at the Gulf Coast Research and Education Center in Balm, Florida
profiles.leadershipSummary: Vance Whitaker joined the program in 2009 and publicly describes taking over when Chandler retired, providing a documented breeder-succession signal.
profiles.primaryContactName: University of Florida strawberry breeding program
profiles.webResearchNotes: Historical University of Florida rollup, not an independent personal-rights target. Keep University, foundation, and licensee roles distinct at the cultivar level.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Timothy E. Williams is the UCR citrus researcher who co-developed Tango and other low-seeded mandarins. The Tango patent records assignment to The Regents of the University of California; UCR remains the institutional licensing contact. The expired U.S. Tango patent does not determine foreign or other-cultivar rights.
profiles.headquarters: Riverside, California, United States (University of California, Riverside)
profiles.leadershipSummary: Williams worked in UCR's team-based citrus program, including with Mikeal Roose. UCR's breeding, Citrus Variety Collection, and technology-transfer structures provide institutional continuity, although a personal retirement or successor notice for Williams was not located.

webResearchSources.label: UCR Tango legacy and patent-expiration update

profiles.webResearchNotes: Cuties and Halos are downstream marketing brands cited in UCR coverage, not evidence that Williams or The Regents own those marks. Preserve cultivar-specific and territory-specific rights.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Terutaka Yoshioka is documented in NARO and predecessor Japanese citrus research and cultivar records. Rights associated with those public breeding programs belong to the named filing-era institution, not to Yoshioka personally; no evidence supports transferring older predecessor rights to him or to a later employer.
profiles.leadershipSummary: Public records show team-based NARO citrus work through recent cultivar publications, but an authoritative current title or named personal successor for Yoshioka was not found.
profiles.webResearchNotes: The canonical Japanese name order is reversed in English-language sources as Terutaka Yoshioka. Identity and NARO relationship are supported; current employment status remains incomplete.

profiles.ownershipSummary: David H. Byrne is the retired Texas A&M stone-fruit and rose breeder who led the peach program beginning in 1983. Texas A&M University System is the documented assignee for Royal Zest Two and administers employee plant IP; Byrne is the breeder, not the personal rightsholder for those program patents.
profiles.headquarters: College Station, Texas, United States (Texas A&M AgriLife)
profiles.leadershipSummary: Byrne is publicly identified as retired. Texas A&M's breeding and IP commercialization structures continue, but no authoritative named successor to his stone-fruit role was located.
profiles.primaryContactName: Texas A&M AgriLife Intellectual Property and Commercialization

webResearchSources.label: Royal Zest Two patent and Texas A&M assignment

profiles.webResearchNotes: The dashboard's apple and cherry aggregation is not supported by the exact-source review and appears malformed or conflated. Confirmed Byrne records concern the Texas A&M peach/nectarine program; do not attach unrelated apple or cherry rights without record-level evidence.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Katalin M. Pakozdi is a breeder on Driscoll strawberry PBR and patent records. Those records name Driscoll's Inc. as holder or assignee; older records may use the predecessor legal name Driscoll Strawberry Associates, Inc. No personal ownership evidence was found.
profiles.headquarters: Watsonville, California, United States (Driscoll's corporate headquarters)
profiles.leadershipSummary: Recent DrisStraw filings show an active, multi-breeder corporate program with institutional continuity; no personal succession event was found.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Roll into the filing-era Driscoll legal entity shown for each cultivar. Preserve predecessor-name history and do not infer personal title from breeder attribution.

profiles.ownershipType: historical public-sector breeder with a potentially conflated profile
profiles.ownershipSummary: Records bearing Tadayuki Haniuda's name occur in Japanese public breeding work, including the former Fruit Tree Research Station/NIFTS lineage and Nagano Prefecture teams. Rights must remain with the public institution named on each patent or PBR record; the available sources do not prove that every variety in the aggregated profile came from one employer.
profiles.headquarters: Japan; no single current headquarters can be assigned across the aggregated institutional records
profiles.leadershipSummary: NARO and Nagano Prefecture both have continuing institutional breeding structures, but no current title or personal successor for Haniuda was located.

webResearchSources.label: Sansa patent and Fruit Tree Research Station assignment

profiles.webResearchNotes: Split and verify by denomination, application number, breeder team, and applicant. In particular, do not roll Nagano Prefecture varieties into NARO merely because other Haniuda records arise from a NARO predecessor.

profiles.ownershipType: corporate blueberry breeder and program manager
profiles.ownershipSummary: Peter Stefan Boches is publicly identified as a blueberry breeder and breeding-program manager at Fall Creek Farm & Nursery. Many FC/FCM selections list Fall Creek as applicant, but inventor records also show cultivar-specific applicants such as Conard-Pyle; those historical rights must not be reassigned wholesale to Fall Creek.
profiles.headquarters: 39318 Jasper-Lowell Road, Lowell, Oregon 97452, United States (Fall Creek home office)
profiles.leadershipSummary: Fall Creek operates a team-based global R&D program, giving institutional continuity beyond one breeder; no personal succession event for Boches was found.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Use cultivar-level applicant and assignment records. Fall Creek employment is verified, but employment alone does not transfer the Conard-Pyle or other third-party applications in the historical portfolio.

profiles.ownershipSummary: The common romanized name Makoto Okada appears in records spanning unrelated strawberry, peach, cherry, apple, and blueberry programs. Public records reviewed do not provide a stable shared address, employer, applicant, or breeder team sufficient to establish that these entries are one individual, so no employer or rightsholder rollup is assigned.
profiles.headquarters: Unresolved; no single location can be tied reliably to the aggregated records
profiles.leadershipSummary: No unique employer, leadership role, or succession signal can be established until the records are split.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Do not guess. Resolve each row using crop, denomination, registration or application number, applicant, breeder-team names, and address; create separate profiles only after those identifiers converge.

profiles.ownershipType: private agricultural partnership and fruit nursery
profiles.ownershipSummary: GAEC des Pepinieres Gromolard is a French groupement agricole d'exploitation en commun and fruit nursery, not an individual breeder. Its official site and catalog present a multi-generation nursery and a proprietary peach-selection portfolio; the GAEC itself is the relevant operating and breeder entity unless a specific PBR record names another holder.
profiles.headquarters: 32 Route de la Saigne, 69510 Messimy, France
profiles.leadershipSummary: The official company history describes three generations of continuity. No authoritative public named successor or current ownership split was found.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Treat as an operating nursery/company profile. The listed selection codes are portfolio evidence, not a claim that each is a registered trademark. Verify PBR holder and commercialization rights cultivar by cultivar.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Hideaki Yaegaki is a NARO stone-fruit breeder and researcher. NARO is the institutional owner or licensing authority for NARO cultivars; joint or prefectural releases, including Yamagata collaborations, must retain their separately named public rightsholders.
profiles.leadershipSummary: Publications through 2024 show active team-based NARO research, providing institutional continuity; no personal succession event was found.
profiles.webResearchNotes: The canonical Japanese name order appears as Hideaki Yaegaki in English sources. Preserve NARO, prefectural, and joint-program ownership separately for each cultivar.

profiles.ownershipType: individual breeder linked to a private Mexican genetics program
profiles.ownershipSummary: Official Moroccan PBR materials name Aurelio Hernandez Bautista as a breeder and Splendor Produce as applicant for cultivars including Erendira, Yunuhen, and SP Erandy. Mexican records indicate that title varies by cultivar and can involve Splendor Produce or Mario Alejandro Andrade Cardenas; no evidence supports treating Aurelio as the personal owner of the full portfolio.
profiles.headquarters: No verified personal headquarters; associated Splendor Produce program operates from Los Reyes, Michoacan, Mexico
profiles.leadershipSummary: Splendor describes an institutional Mexican genetics program begun after the company was founded in 2007, but Aurelio's exact employment title and any personal succession arrangement were not found.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Identity as a breeder is supported, but exact employment and cultivar-level ownership are not uniform. Preserve Splendor and Mario Alejandro Andrade Cardenas as separate filing-era rightsholders where each is named.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Lluvia V. Gutierrez is a breeder on Driscoll raspberry patents. Historical records name Driscoll Strawberry Associates, Inc. and newer records name Driscoll's, Inc. as assignee; the breeder is not shown as personal owner, and each filing should retain its legal entity name.
profiles.headquarters: Watsonville, California, United States (Driscoll's corporate headquarters)
profiles.leadershipSummary: Patent activity extending into 2026 shows active participation in a team-based corporate breeding program and institutional continuity.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Roll into the applicable filing-era Driscoll entity; do not rewrite older Driscoll Strawberry Associates assignments solely because the current company uses Driscoll's, Inc.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Miguel H. Ahumada is an inventor on DrisRaspOne and DrisRaspSix, both assigned to Driscoll Strawberry Associates, Inc. The patent trail supports a historical Driscoll breeding relationship but not personal ownership or current employment.
profiles.headquarters: Watsonville, California, United States (filing-era Driscoll corporate program); inventor addresses in the cited patents list Ojai, California
profiles.leadershipSummary: The cited activity is historical, from patents filed and granted between 2008 and 2014. Driscoll's continuing corporate breeding program supplies institutional continuity, but Ahumada's current role or personal successor is not verified.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Use the filing-era assignee name Driscoll Strawberry Associates, Inc. for these patents. Do not infer that a current Driscoll contact path proves current employment.

profiles.ownershipType: deceased private breeder and breeding-company founder
profiles.ownershipSummary: Marvin L. Nies was a private California fruit breeder and founder of Proprietary Fruit Varieties, Inc. His Giant Red U.S. plant patent was filed with an individual assignee, while 2025 Swiss PBR notices identify Proprietary Fruits Varieties (Contracts), LLC as owner and Nies as initial breeder for later IVU selections. Those later ownership records do not retroactively transfer every historical Nies patent.
profiles.headquarters: Historical breeding ranch and company in Lodi, California, United States; 2025 IVU-related PBR owner address in Las Vegas, Nevada
profiles.leadershipSummary: Nies died in 2015. Meda and IVU state that, beginning in 2009, IVU worked with Nies, his family, and commercial partners to select and develop his material, providing a documented succession and commercialization path.

webResearchSources.label: Giant Red patent and individual assignment
Swiss 2025 PBR notice for IVU selections

profiles.webResearchNotes: Preserve historical individual ownership and later company ownership separately. Nies's breeder credit survives in current IVU applications, but current owner, marketer, and brand roles must be verified for each denomination and territory.
